summary: Descriptive overview of defamation in substantive law: libel vs slander, public figure standard, actual malice.
key_points: libel vs slander is a fundamental concept in tort law.
Legal standards for defamation are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine defamation in litigation.
The doctrine of defamation has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding defamation requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
